free_text: Scattering of alpha particles on 11B nuclei at energies
40 and 50 MeV
English translation of YF,68,1356
- the same data presented in
Subent 002.
U-150M isochronous cyclotron
Scattered particles were detected by
telescope consisting of two silicon counters: a thin
(dE) counter and another (E) of total absorption with
the thicknesses of 30 mu-m and 1 mm, correspondingly.
Overall energy resolution was 400-500 keV and it was
defined mainly by the energy spread in cyclotron beam.
Self supporting foils of 11B with 99% enrichment were
used as the targets. Target thicknesses were from 0.1
to 0.2 mg/cm2. They were determined from the energy
losses of alpha particles with the accuracy of ~ 8%.
Systematic error of cross sections
At measurements in the region of forward
hemisphere statistical error was 1-5%. At large
angles it increased but did not exceed 10%.
No source of uncertainties is given.
Data were presented by authors.
English translation in REFERENCE was added.
ERR-ANALYS in sub 001 was corrected. Sub 003 was
added. Data presentation in sub 002-003 was changed.
SD: Ref. APP/BS,14,719 added. ERR-ANALYS
updated.
